title: Security issues reports in DevOps | SonarQube Server Documentation
content:
# Viewing and managing issues in your DevOps platform

## [Managing security issues in GitHub](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-server/latest/user-guide/issues/security-issues-in-devops-platform/#github "Managing security issues in GitHub")

When you analyze a project in SonarQube, the detected security issues are displayed on the GitHub interface as code scanning alerts [if set up in your system](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-server/latest/devops-platform-integration/github-integration/setting-up-at-global-level/report-security-alerts/ "if set up in your system"). When you change the status of a security issue in the SonarQube interface that status change is immediately reflected in the GitHub interface. Similarly, if you change the status of a code scanning alert in GitHub, that change is reflected in SonarQube.

To view and manage your code scanning alerts:

1. In GitHub, go to your repository's **Security** > **Code scanning alerts** tab.

2. Select **View alerts** to see the full list.

![](https://docs.sonarsource.com/Base64-Image-Removed)

![Screenshot that shows the code scanning alerts section in GitHub. ](https://assets-eu-01.kc-usercontent.com:443/b1eeb429-d9e0-0100-be87-468f6802040a/ee975260-8b54-455d-a585-6ae499b60470/sonar-github-code-scanning-alerts.png?w=1462&h=644&auto=format&fit=crop "Screenshot that shows the code scanning alerts section in GitHub. ")

## [Viewing the security issues in GitLab](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-server/latest/user-guide/issues/security-issues-in-devops-platform/#gitlab "Viewing the security issues in GitLab")

When you analyze a project in SonarQube Server, the detected security issues are displayed on the GitLab interface as security vulnerabilities [if set up in GitLab CI/CD](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-server/latest/devops-platform-integration/gitlab-integration/setting-up-at-project-level/ "if set up in GitLab CI/CD"). When you change the status of a security issue in the SonarQube Server interface that status change is immediately reflected in the GitLab interface.

If you change the status of a security vulnerability in GitLab, that change is *not* reflected in SonarQube Server.

To view the security vulnerabilities:

- Go to the **GitLab** > **Vulnerability** report page.

If your issues appear duplicated (it may be the case after the modification of a file), we recommend using the **Activity** > **Still detected** filter.

## [Viewing the issues detected on a pull request in Azure DevOps](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-server/latest/user-guide/issues/security-issues-in-devops-platform/#azure-devops "Viewing the issues detected on a pull request in Azure DevOps")

When you run a SonarQube Server analysis for a pull request, each SonarQube issue is displayed as a comment on the Azure DevOps pull request. If the Azure DevOps instance is configured correctly and you change the status of an issue in SonarQube Server, that status change is immediately reflected in the Azure DevOps interface.
